perturbation.original_text: 8. CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. "Confidential Information" means all documents, software, reports, data, records, forms, conversations and other materials obtained by Contractor from Company in the course of performing any Services (including, but not limited to, Company records and information). Notwithstanding the foregoing, Confidential Information does not include information which: (i) is or becomes publicly known through no wrongful act of Contractor; or (ii) is independently developed by Contractor without benefit of Company's
perturbation.changed_text: 8. CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. "Confidential Information" means all documents, software, reports, data, records, forms, conversations and other materials obtained by Contractor from Company in the course of performing any Services (including, but not limited to, Company records and information). All information disclosed under this agreement is confidential information. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Confidential Information does not include information which: (i) is or becomes publicly known through no wrongful act of Contractor; or (ii) is independently developed by Contractor without benefit of Company's
perturbation.explanation: The added sentence 'All information disclosed under this agreement is confidential information' broadens the definition in direct conflict with the exceptions that follow immediately, i.e., that certain information is *not* considered Confidential Information, specifically, information that is or becomes publicly known or is independently developed. This modification makes the scope of 'Confidential Information' ambiguous, leading to potential disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. "Confidential Information" means all documents, software, reports, data, records, forms, conversations and other materials obtained by Contractor from Company in the course of performing any Services (including, but not limited to, Company records and information). All information disclosed under this agreement is confidential information. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Confidential Information does not include information which: (i) is or becomes publicly known through no wrongful act of Contractor; or (ii) is independently developed by Contractor without benefit of Company's Confidential Information. Contractor shall not use or disclose to any person, firm or entity any Confidential Information without Company's express, prior written permission; provided, however, that notwithstanding the foregoing, Contractor may disclose Confidential Information to the extent that it is required to be disclosed pursuant to a statutory or regulatory provision or court order.
perturbation.justification: The added sentence 'All information disclosed under this agreement is confidential information' directly contradicts the exceptions that follow, namely that information that becomes publicly known or is independently developed is *not* Confidential Information. This creates a clear in-text contradiction because the modified text states that ALL information is confidential, while simultaneously stating that SOME information is NOT confidential. This makes the scope of 'Confidential Information' unclear and leads to ambiguity and potential disputes, in direct violation of the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Contractor represents and warrants to Company that Contractor has full authority and sufficient rights, except for rights respecting programs, data and materials provided by Company or identified by Contractor as furnished to Company by third-party vendors, to grant and convey the rights granted to Company under Paragraph 4 hereof;
perturbation.changed_text: Contractor represents and warrants to Company that Contractor has full authority and sufficient rights to grant and convey the rights granted to Company under Paragraph 4 hereof; Contractor also represents that the rights of programs, data and materials provided by Company or identified by Contractor as furnished to Company by third-party vendors are not included in the rights being granted.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase 'except for rights respecting programs, data and materials provided by Company or identified by Contractor as furnished to Company by third-party vendors', and subsequently including those rights into the text, now the sentence Contractor represents and warrants to Company that Contractor has full authority and sufficient rights to grant and convey the rights granted to Company under Paragraph 4 hereof sounds contradictory. It's contradictory because the initial representation lacks the explicit exclusion, potentially creating an ambiguity about what rights are effectively being transferred, thus creating in-text contradiction within the new sentance added into 5(b).
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) Contractor represents and warrants to Company that Contractor has full authority and sufficient rights to grant and convey the rights granted to Company under Paragraph 4 hereof; Contractor also represents that the rights of programs, data and materials provided by Company or identified by Contractor as furnished to Company by third-party vendors are not included in the rights being granted.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the original representation by removing the explicit exclusion of rights related to third-party vendor materials. This creates a contradiction because the Contractor now represents having full authority over all rights granted in Paragraph 4, while the added sentence states that the rights of programs, data, and materials provided by Company or identified by Contractor as furnished to Company by third-party vendors are not included in the rights being granted. This dual statement creates an in-text contradiction within the same section (b) as now the Contractor seems to be granting rights, that they are also explicitly stating they are not granting. This leads to confusion and uncertainty regarding what rights are being conveyed, directly causing in-text contradiction as defined by 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction'.
